mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration and recession risk, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative mean 60-day returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 300.0, persistent inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL > 300.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and real GDP confirms synchronized economic expansion, boosting corporate profit expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and GDPC1 signal yields negative mean 60-day returns, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample models.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a decline in total nonfarm payrolls signals labor market cooling that outweighs growth expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines during tightening cycles produce positive 20-day returns, or the regime filter fails to improve predictive accuracy over unconditional models.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in advance retail sales reflect immediate consumer spending strength, providing a direct positive earnings catalyst that generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or the signal shows negative predictive power in > 50% of monthly observations.
